workflow.name: Monitor supplier financial distress and risk with Bright Data, OpenRouter and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## Description
This workflow automatically scans companies for signs of financial distress across filings, insolvency registers, and financial news.  
It helps procurement, credit, and risk teams detect early warning signals before a supplier or partner defaults.

## Overview
This workflow uses Bright Data to scrape financial filings, insolvency registers, and news sources for distress signals like bankruptcy, restructuring, or payment defaults.  

AI classifies the type and severity of distress, applies probability weighting and confidence guardrails, then generates structured business decisions — including:

- Supplier Monitoring risk status  
- Onboarding Approval recommendations  
- Portfolio Exposure classifications  

All outputs are logged into Google Sheets for tracking and auditability.

## Tools Used
- **n8n**: Automation platform orchestrating the workflow  
- **Bright Data**: Scrapes filings, insolvency registers, and financial news without getting blocked  
- **OpenRouter**: AI-powered distress classification, risk scoring, and business decision generation  
- **Google Sheets**: Logs supplier risk status, onboarding decisions, portfolio exposure, and errors  

## How to Install

### 1. Import the Workflow
Download the `.json` file and import it into your n8n instance.

### 2. Configure Bright Data
Add your Bright Data API credentials to all Bright Data nodes.

### 3. Configure OpenRouter
Add your OpenRouter API key for AI distress classification and decision generation.

### 4. Set Up Google Sheets
Create a spreadsheet following the **"Google Sheets Setup"** sticky note inside the workflow.  
Connect each Google Sheets node to your document.

### 5. Customize
Edit the configuration node to define:
- Target company  
- Country  
- Risk indicators  
- Monitoring scope  

## Use Cases

### Procurement Teams
Monitor supplier financial health and get alerts before disruptions hit your supply chain.

### Credit Risk Analysts
Screen new vendors or partners for bankruptcy signals and insolvency red flags.

### Onboarding Workflows
Automate go/no-go decisions for new supplier or partner approvals.

### Portfolio Managers
Track financial exposure across your vendor or investment portfolio.

### Finance Teams
Detect early signs of distress in key business relationships before they become critical.

## Connect with Me
- Website: https://www.nofluff.online  
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@YaronBeen/videos  
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yaronbeen/  
- Get Bright Data: https://get.brightdata.com/1tndi4600b25  
  *(Using this link supports my free workflows with a small commission)*

## Tags
#n8n #automation #brightdata #webscraping #creditrisk #financialdistress #riskmanagement  
#suppliermonitoring #supplychainrisk #insolvency #bankruptcy #duediligence #vendorscreening  
#portfoliorisk #financialanalysis #n8nworkflow #workflow #nocode #businessintelligence  
#riskassessment #creditanalysis #procurementautomation #supplierrisk #financialmonitoring #earlywarning

workflow.name: 2. Detecting Financial Distress & Credit Risk with Bright Data & n8n

documentId.cachedResultName: 2. Bright Data Financial Distress & Credit Risk Intelligence Engine

parameters.text:
=Analyze the following financial distress signals:

{{ $json.distressSignals }}

Instructions:
- Base your decision ONLY on the provided signals.
- If signals are weak or limited, assign LOW probability.
- Be conservative in scoring.
- Do not assume missing information.

Classify:
1. Primary distress type
2. Probability (Low / Medium / High)
3. Numerical risk score (0–100)
4. Short explanation (1–2 lines max)

Respond ONLY in strictly valid JSON format:

{
  "distressType": "",
  "probability": "",
  "riskScore": 0,
  "explanation": ""
}

options.systemMessage:
You are a financial credit risk classification assistant.

You MUST:
- Return strictly valid JSON.
- Return no extra text outside JSON.
- Be conservative and risk-aware.
- Never invent facts not present in the signals.

If signals are minimal, classify as LOW probability.

You are a senior financial risk intelligence engine.

Your task is to transform structured financial risk analysis data into three business-ready outputs:

1) Supplier Monitoring Risk View
2) Onboarding Credit Decision Snapshot
3) Portfolio Exposure Feed

Rules:
- Return STRICTLY valid JSON.
- No extra commentary.
- No markdown.
- No explanations outside JSON.
- Be conservative and risk-aware.
- Base all decisions ONLY on provided scores and confidence values.
- Do not invent data.
- Maintain numerical integrity of provided scores.
- Use structured decision logic.

2. Bright Data Financial Distress & Credit Risk Intelligence Engine

parameters.content:
## Financial Distress & Credit Risk Intelligence Engine

This workflow scans a company across financial filings, insolvency registers, and financial news to detect early distress signals.

It:

- Collects multi-source financial risk indicators
- Detects bankruptcy, insolvency, restructuring & default signals
- Classifies financial distress using AI
- Applies probability weighting and confidence guardrails
- Converts analysis into business-ready credit decisions

Outputs include:
• Supplier Monitoring Risk Status  
• Onboarding Approval Recommendation  
• Portfolio Exposure Classification  

What you need:
- Bright Data account (for web scraping)
- OpenRouter API key (for AI analysis)
- Google Sheets (for output dashboards)

Built for conservative, signal-based risk intelligence.

## Multi-Source Financial Signal Collection

Collects and consolidates financial distress indicators from multiple verified public sources for early credit risk detection.

- Scrapes financial filings for bankruptcy or restructuring disclosures  
- Monitors insolvency registers for liquidation or court filings  
- Extracts distress-related financial news mentions  
- Detects filing, insolvency, and news-based distress signals  
- Merges signals into a unified structured dataset  
- Logs scraping failures for monitoring and debugging  

Creates a reliable multi-source foundation for downstream financial distress classification.

## AI Financial Distress Classification & Risk Scoring

Analyzes detected financial distress signals using AI to estimate credit risk exposure and refine scoring reliability.

- Categorizes and normalizes detected distress signals  
- Prepares structured inputs for AI evaluation  
- Identifies primary financial distress type  
- Assigns probability of distress occurrence  
- Generates conservative risk score  
- Applies probability-based score weighting  
- Validates AI-generated structured output  
- Applies confidence guardrails to adjust final risk score  

Ensures reliable financial risk classification based on multi-source signal strength.

## Confidence Guardrails & Business Decisioning

Refines financial risk scores using confirmation logic and converts validated insights into actionable business decisions.

- Applies confidence guardrails to adjust final risk score  
- Uses multi-source confirmation for validation  
- Generates supplier monitoring risk status  
- Produces onboarding approval recommendation  
- Categorizes portfolio exposure level  
- Logs supplier monitoring decisions  
- Records onboarding and exposure insights  

Transforms validated financial signals into operational credit risk decisions.

## Setup Instructions

Before running this workflow, configure the following credentials in n8n:

**1. Bright Data API** — Required for web scraping
- Sign up at brightdata.com
- Create an API token
- Add as "BrightData account" credential in n8n

**2. OpenRouter API** — Required for AI classification
- Sign up at openrouter.ai
- Generate an API key
- Add as "OpenRouter account" credential in n8n

**3. Google Sheets OAuth** — Required for output logging
- Connect your Google account in n8n
- See the **Google Sheets Setup** sticky note for required tabs and columns
- Update each Google Sheets node to point to your new spreadsheet

After connecting credentials, edit the configuration node to set your target companies, URLs, or parameters.

## Input & Risk Configuration

Initial configuration block where company details and financial risk indicators are defined for distress analysis.

- Initiates financial risk scan  
- Sets company name for evaluation  
- Defines country of incorporation  
- Specifies financial distress indicators to monitor  
- Validates input parameters before signal collection  

Enables targeted financial risk detection based on configurable inputs.

## Google Sheets Setup

Create a Google Spreadsheet with 4 tabs and add these column headers in row 1:

**Tab: Supplier Monitoring**
companyName | country | riskStatus | distressType | probability | confidenceScore | lastUpdated | explanation

**Tab: Onboarding Snapshot Sheet**
CompanyName | Country | approvalRecommendation | distressType | confidenceScore | TimeStamp

**Tab: portfolioExposure**
countryName | company | exposureCategory | portfolioRiskWeight

**Tab: BD log error**
errorSource | errorMessage | errorCode | timestamp | hasError

After creating the spreadsheet, update each Google Sheets node to point to your document and select the matching tab.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
